sanskrit:
काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः।
क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा॥
transliteration:
kāṅkṣantaḥ karmaṇāṃ siddhiṃ yajanta iha devatāḥ |
kṣipraṃ hi mānuṣe loke siddhir bhavati karmajā ||
literal_meaning: Desiring success in (fruitive) actions, people here worship the demigods; for quickly indeed in the human world success born of action is obtained.
contextual_meaning: Krishna exposes the short-cut mindset: when anxious for fast, visible pay-offs we outsource our agency to powerful figures—celestial or human—rather than owning the deeper, slower work of inner growth.
psychological_state: External locus of control; magical thinking that a powerful other will rescue me from uncertainty.
trigger_scenario: Checking your phone every few minutes to see if the influencer you DM’d for career advice has replied, while your own long-term project sits untouched.
anchor_text: True success grows from inner work, not quick favors from powerful figures.
